_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice anchored at 100 in 2015, currently stands at 132. This indicates a 32% increase in the overall cost of these commodities since 2015. The rise has been uneven across the items, reflecting a recent climb influenced by specific supply shocks. The index summarizes global commodity prices and serves as a snapshot of these essential breakfast-related goods over time.
notes.movers: Among the components, coffee prices have surged the most, up 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 22%. Wheat and sugar have seen more modest increases of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price changes are primarily driven by weather events and crop diseases, such as citrus greening affecting orange juice and frost or drought conditions in arabica coffee regions, rather than general inflation. It is important to note that soft commodities like these are traded in relatively thin markets, making prices more sensitive to environmental factors.
notes.why: The key takeaway is that price movements in a few weather-sensitive crops are driving the overall increase in the breakfast basket index. Because these markets are thin and conditions like weather and disease can persist, elevated input prices may remain in place for an extended period. This dynamic underscores the vulnerability of certain agricultural commodities to environmental risks rather than broad economic inflation trends.
